data.title: Bitcoin retains $70,000 as oil price jumps 8% on Iran tension, impacting global markets.
data.description: At the end of the week, Bitcoin maintained a value of $70,000 amid changing market conditions sparked by the disruption of negotiations between the US and Iran and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: Michael Saylor, Strategy's head, hints at further Bitcoin buy; 105 transactions done since 2020 through debt and equity.
data.description: Since 2020, the company has finalized 105 Bitcoin transactions and is now taking a unique approach by consistently acquiring more BTC through corporate debt and equity funding.

data.title: Senator Cynthia Lummis warns US must pass the CLARITY Act before 2030 to secure financial future amid crypto regulation uncertainty.
data.description: Senator Cynthia Lummis, a strong supporter of cryptocurrency, emphasized the importance of not delaying the CLARITY Act to secure the country's financial stability.

data.title: What 4 Million K-12 Submissions Reveal About the Hidden Work of Grading
data.description: Agilix Labs revealed that BusyBee, their AI grading assistant operating on Amazon Bedrock from AWS, has efficiently evaluated assessments across over 600 school domains. The AI-powered assessment system at production scale provides a glimpse into the future of education technology. This news was shared from OREM, Utah on April 13, 2026.

data.title: McDonald’s will introduce fruity drinks in response to rivals as cold drinks sales surge.
data.description: Next month, the popular fast-food chain will introduce fruit-flavored drinks to its menu in response to the increasing popularity of cold beverages over hot ones. This decision comes as competitors such as Dunkin’ and Starbucks are reporting higher sales of cold drinks compared to hot beverages.

data.title: Securing AI becomes top priority as CIOs rank AI alongside malware, ransomware and phishing as major cyber risk
data.description: A survey of Chief Information Officers (CIOs) reveals that they consider coping with a cyber skills deficit and addressing vulnerabilities stemming from shadow AI as crucial challenges. Almost half of CIOs express regret over the invention of AI. A staggering 77% of organizations encountered cybersecurity breaches in the last year, with AI-driven threats surpassing traditional security measures.

data.title: Cloudcomputing buys UK's Innovate IT to scale international growth and cyber capabilities
data.description: A recent acquisition by Cloudcomputing, a specialist in identity and cybersecurity, is set to significantly enhance business growth in 2026. The acquisition of Innovate IT Ltd, a UK-based company, aims to expand Cloudcomputing's delivery capacity by four times in both the UK and US markets. The acquisition is expected to pave the way for increased global presence and better service offerings in the cybersecurity industry.

data.title: Despite facing sanctions, Iran bolstered its economy, transitioning away from dependency on oil exports amidst high inflation and unemployment.
data.description: Before the outbreak of the war, the nation grappled with significant challenges including high inflation rates, reaching an average of 15% annually, and high unemployment rates, with around 12% of the population unemployed. These economic difficulties were exacerbated by widespread unrest and social discontent. Despite being known primarily as an oil exporter, the nation faced multifaceted issues that went beyond its role in the global energy market.

data.title: Business Travel Market Expected to Reach USD 2,095.4 billion by 2031, at a CAGR of 9.5%: Allied Market Research
data.description: A surge in tourism promotion efforts, improved accessibility of transport facilities, and a rise in infrastructural developments are anticipated to drive growth in the business travel market. In a recent report released by Allied Market Research in WILMINGTON, Del. on April 13, 2026, it was revealed that these factors are projected to lead to a significant increase in the sector. The report outlines key statistics and trends shaping the market, with a focus on key growth drivers.

data.title: Tryg - Transactions in connection with share buyback programme
data.description: Tryg A/S revealed on January 22, 2026, their intention to start a share buyback initiative totaling DKK 1.0 billion. The buyback follows guidelines of EU Market Abuse Regulation and EU Regulation no. 596/2014, as well as Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052. The programme, compliant with the Safe Harbour Regulation, is set to conclude by May 13, 2026.
